answer:
To find the short-term liability of \$33.4 million as a percentage of the long-term receivable of \$95.3 million, use the formula:

\[
\text{Percentage} = \left( \frac{33.4}{95.3} \right) \times 100 \approx 35.03\%
\]

Thus, the short-term liability is approximately **\boxed{35.03\%}** of the long-term receivable.
